brief_justification: Paper A is a semiclassical spectral-counting result (a Weyl law for quasinormal modes of a Schwarzschild black hole via a new pseudodifferential calculus, elliptic theory, complex scaling, and uniform resolvent estimates near threshold energy), while paper B is a machine-learning training recipe (Test-Time Training that stores a recurrent state of fast weights updated by gradient descent to scale visuomotor context for robot policies); there is no common governing process, threshold, or transferable structure to map role-for-role, only the generic and non-machinery overlap of 'operators/estimates'.
